zh.lifeSummary: 當William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick於大約1238年出生在英國瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素時，他的父親William de Beauchamp25歲，母親Isabel Mauduit25歲。他於1269年與Maud FitzJohn在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他於1114年住在英國武斯特斯爾。他於1268年被列為high sheriff of worcestershire。他去世於1298年6月5日在英國武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素享年61歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick于大约1238年出生在英国瓦維其可爾沃里克沃里克 卡素时，他的父亲William de Beauchamp25岁，母亲Isabel Mauduit25岁。他于1269年与Maud FitzJohn在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他于1114年住在英国武斯特斯爾。他于1268年被列为high sheriff of worcestershire。他去世于1298年6月5日在英国武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素享年61岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick was born about 1238, in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, his father, William de Beauchamp, was 25 and his mother, Isabel Mauduit, was 25. He married Maud FitzJohn in 1269, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 8 daughters. He lived in Worcestershire, England in 1114. In 1268, at the age of 31, his occupation is listed as high sheriff of worcestershire. He died on 5 June 1298, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick est né environ 1238, à Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Isabel Mauduit, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Maud FitzJohn en 1269, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 8 filles. Il a vécu à Worcestershire, Angleterre en 1114. En 1268, à l’âge de 31 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est high sheriff of worcestershire. Il est décédé le 5 juin 1298, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick um 1238 in Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Beauchamp 25 und seine Mutter Isabel Mauduit 25. Er hat 1269 in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Maud FitzJohn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 8 Töchter. 1114 hat er in Worcestershire, England gelebt. 1268, als er 31 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als high sheriff of worcestershire aufgeführt. Er starb am 5. Juni 1298 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England und wurde in Worcestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick è nato il circa 1238 nel Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, England, il suo padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Mauduit, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Maud FitzJohn nel 1269, nel Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 8 figlie. Vissuto a Worcestershire, England nel 1114. Nel 1268, all'età di 31 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come high sheriff of worcestershire. Morì il 5 giugno 1298, nel Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwickが約1238年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックワーウィック カストルで生まれた時，父William de Beauchampは25歳，母Isabel Mauduitは25歳であった。1269年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルでMaud FitzJohnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1114年にはイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアに住んでいた。1268年，31歳の時，職業はhigh sheriff of worcestershireと記録されている。彼は1298年6月5日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルで61歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick(이)가 대략 1238년 영국 워윅실르 워윅 워윅 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Mauduit(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1269년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 Maud FitzJohn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 1114년도에 영국 워스스터실르에서 살았다. 1268년, 31세의 나이로 그의 직업은 high sheriff of worcestershire이었다. 그는 1298년 6월 5일 영국 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick nasceu aproximadamente 1238, em Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Mauduit, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud FitzJohn em 1269, em Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele viveu em Worcestershire, Inglaterra em 1114. Em 1268, com 31 anos, sua profissão aparece como high sheriff of worcestershire. Ele faleceu em 5 de junho de 1298, em Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick родился около 1238 г. в местности варвикк кастл, варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Mauduit, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud FitzJohn в 1269 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 8 дочерей. Он жил в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в 1114 году. В 1268 году, в возрасте 31 год,  он работал по профессии high sheriff of worcestershire. Он умер 5 июня 1298 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick nació aproximadamente 1238, en Warwick Castle, Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Beauchamp, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Isabel Mauduit, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Maud FitzJohn en 1269, en Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 8 hijas. Vivió en Worcestershire, Inglaterra en 1114. En 1268, a la edad de 31 años, su ocupación registrada era high sheriff of worcestershire. Murió el 5 de junio de 1298, en Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
